sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 103, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 102 actions; a strong score needs about 26 or fewer. You are 3.9x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The state after SPACE is:
- White pixel at [27, 21] - on the yellow cross
- Yellow cross: centered at [27, 21] with horizontal bar at row 27, vertical bar at col 21
- Blue cross: centered at [45, 36] with horizontal bar at row 45, vertical bar at col 36
- Y dots: 4 at [3, 15], [9, 6], [9, 24], [17, 15]
- b dots: 4 at [16, 48], [24, 40], [24, 53], [35, 48]
This is NOT the initial state. The crosses are at different positions. SPACE seems to reset the dots but not the cross positions. Or maybe the crosses moved when the game over happened.
Looking at the yellow cross structure:
- Vertical: rows 16-26 and 28-38 at col 21 (centered at row 27)
- Horizontal: row 27, cols 10-20 and 22-32 (centered at col 21)
The yellow cross is at [27, 21]. The blue cross is at [45, 36].
For the yellow cross to collect yellow dots:
- [3, 15]: need to move cross to row 3, col 15
- [9, 6]: need to move cross to row 9, col 6
- [9, 24]: need to move cross to row 9, col 24
- [17, 15]: need to move cross to row 17, col 15
The yellow cross moves 3 units per arrow key press. From [27, 21]:
- To reach row 17: (27-17)/3 = 3.33, not an integer. So I can't reach exactly row 17.
- To reach row 9: (27-9)/3 = 6 UP moves.
- To reach row 3: (27-3)/3 = 8 UP moves.
Wait, but the cross has a horizontal bar that spans 11 units on each side. So from center row 27, the horizontal bar is at row 27, spanning cols 10-32. To collect [17, 15], I need the horizontal bar to be at row 17. From row 27, that's (27-17)/3 = 3.33 moves, which doesn't work.
Hmm, but maybe the cross doesn't need to be exactly on the dot. Maybe the cross collects dots that are within its bar range. The horizontal bar spans 11 units on each side, so from center col 21, it spans cols 10-32. The dot at [17, 15] is at col 15, which is within 10-32. But the row is 17, not 27.
Actually, I think the cross collects dots when the cross's bar passes through the dot's position. So for a yellow dot at [row, col], the yellow cross needs to have its horizontal bar at that row (to collect via horizontal bar) or its vertical bar at that col (to collect via vertical bar).
Let me reconsider. The yellow cross has:
- Horizontal bar at row 27, spanning cols 10-32
- Vertical bar at col 21, spanning rows 16-38
For [17, 15]: row 17 is within 16-38 (vertical bar range), and col 15 is not 21. So the vertical bar doesn't cover this dot. The horizontal bar is at row 27, not row 17. So this dot is not collected.
For [9, 6]: row 9 is not within 16-38, col 6 is not 21. Not collected.
For [9, 24]: row 9 is not within 16-38, col 24 is not 21. Not collected.
For [3, 15]: row 3 is not within 16-38, col 15 is not 21. Not collected.
So none of the yellow dots are currently collected by the yellow cross.
To collect yellow dots, I need to move the yellow cross so its bars pass through the dots. The yellow cross is at [27, 21].
Let me think about the optimal path:
1. [17, 15]: Move UP so horizontal bar is at row 17. From row 27, that's (27-17)/3 = 3.33 moves. Not an integer.
   - Or move LEFT so vertical bar is at col 15. From col 21, that's (21-15)/3 = 2 LEFT moves.
   - After 2 LEFT, center is at [27, 15], vertical bar at col 15 spans rows 16-38. This covers [17, 15]!
2. [9, 6]: After collecting [17, 15], move UP so horizontal bar is at row 9. From row 27, that's (27-9)/3 = 6 UP moves.
   - After 6 UP, center is at [9, 15], horizontal bar at row 9 spans cols 4-26. This covers [9, 6] (col 6) and [9, 24] (col 24)!
3. [3, 15]: After collecting [9, 6] and [9, 24], move UP so horizontal bar is at row 3. From row 9, that's (9-3)/3 = 2 UP moves.
   - After 2 UP, center is at [3, 15], horizontal bar at row 3 spans cols -8 to 26. Wait, cols -8 is off the board!
   - Actually, the horizontal bar spans 11 units on each side, so from col 15, it spans cols 4-26. Row 3 is within the board.
   - But wait, the vertical bar at col 15 spans rows -8 to 14 from center row 3. Row -8 is off the board!
   - This might cause game over again.
Hmm, I need to be careful about boundaries. The board is 64x64 (rows 0-63, cols 0-63). The cross is 23x23 (11 units on each side from center). So the center must be at least 11 units from the edge to avoid going off the board.
For the yellow cross:
- Center row must be in [11, 52] to keep the cross on the board (rows 0-63).
- Center col must be in [11, 52] to keep the cross on the board (cols 0-63).
Wait, but earlier I moved the cross to row 3 and it caused game over. Let me reconsider.
Actually, looking at the yellow cross structure, the vertical bar spans rows 16-26 and 28-38 from center row 27. That's 11 rows above and 11 rows below the center (with a gap at the center row). So the cross extends 11 rows above and below the center.
For the cross to stay on the board:
- Center row must be in [11, 63-11] = [11, 52].
- Center col must be in [11, 63-11] = [11, 52].
So I can't move the yellow cross to row 3 or col 3. The minimum center row/col is 11.
Let me reconsider the yellow dot positions:
- [3, 15]: row 3 is too high for the cross to reach (min center row is 11, so min horizontal bar row is 11-11=0... wait, that's not right).
Actually, the horizontal bar is at the center row. So if the center is at row 11, the horizontal bar is at row 11. The dot at [3, 15] is at row 3, which is 8 rows above the horizontal bar. The vertical bar at col 15 would span rows 0-22 from center [11, 15]. Row 3 is within 0-22, so the vertical bar would cover [3, 15]!
Wait, let me recalculate. The yellow cross has:
- Horizontal bar at center row, spanning 11 cols on each side.
- Vertical bar at center col, spanning 11 rows on each side.
So from center [27, 21]:
- Horizontal bar: row 27, cols 10-32.
- Vertical bar: col 21, rows 16-38.
From center [11, 15]:
- Horizontal bar: row 11, cols 4-22.
- Vertical bar: col 15, rows 0-22.
The dot at [3, 15] is at row 3, col 15. The vertical bar at col 15 spans rows 0-22, which includes row 3. So the vertical bar would cover [3, 15]!
So the plan for collecting yellow dots:
1. Move LEFT 2 times to center at [27, 15]. Vertical bar at col 15 covers [17, 15].
2. Move UP 6 times to center at [9, 15]. Horizontal bar at row 9 covers [9, 6] and [9, 24].
3. Move UP 2 more times to center at [7, 15]. Vertical bar at col 15 spans rows -4 to 18. Row 3 is within -4 to 18, so vertical bar covers [3, 15].
Wait, but center row 7 means the vertical bar spans rows 7-11=-4 to 7+11=18. Row -4 is off the board, but the dot at [3, 15] is at row 3, which is within the board. Would this cause game over?
Earlier, when I moved UP from [24, 15], the game over happened after 2 UP moves (center at [18, 15]). Let me check: from [24, 15], 2 UP moves would put the center at [24-6, 15] = [18, 15]. The vertical bar would span rows 7-29. This is within the board.
But the game over happened. Maybe the game over was caused by something else, like the cross going off the board in a different direction.
Actually, looking back at the history, the game over happened after I moved the yellow cross UP from [24, 15]. After 2 UP moves, the center would be at [18, 15]. But the game over happened. This suggests that the boundary is tighter than I thought.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the cross extends 13 units on each side (not 11). Looking at the blue cross segmentation:
- Blue cross: pixels=13, rows 11-23, cols 48-48 (vertical bar, 13 pixels)
- Blue cross: pixels=13, rows 24-24, cols 35-47 (horizontal bar left, 13 pixels)
- Blue cross: pixels=13, rows 24-24, cols 49-61 (horizontal bar right, 13 pixels)
- Blue cross: pixels=13, rows 25-37, cols 48-48 (vertical bar, 13 pixels)
So the blue cross has bars that are 13 pixels long on each side of the center. The center is at [24, 48]. The vertical bar spans rows 11-37 (24-13=11, 24+13=37). The horizontal bar spans cols 35-61 (48-13=35, 48+13=61).
For the yellow cross:
- Yellow: pixels=11, rows 16-26, cols 21-21 (vertical bar top, 11 pixels)
- Yellow: pixels=11, rows 27-27, cols 10-20 (horizontal bar left, 11 pixels)
- Yellow: pixels=11, rows 27-27, cols 22-32 (horizontal bar right, 11 pixels)
- Yellow: pixels=11, rows 28-38, cols 21-21 (vertical bar bottom, 11 pixels)
So the yellow cross has bars that are 11 pixels long on each side of the center. The center is at [27, 21]. The vertical bar spans rows 16-38 (27-11=16, 27+11=38). The horizontal bar spans cols 10-32 (21-11=10, 21+11=32).
So the yellow cross extends 11 units on each side. For the cross to stay on the board:
- Center row must be in [11, 63-11] = [11, 52].
- Center col must be in [11, 63-11] = [11, 52].
From [27, 21], I can move:
- UP: to row 27-3=24, 21, 18, 15, 12, 9... but 9 is less than 11, so the minimum center row is 11 (or maybe 12 to be safe).
- DOWN: to row 27+3=30, 33, 36, 39, 42, 45, 48, 51... 51 is within [11, 52].
- LEFT: to col 21-3=18, 15, 12, 9... 9 is less than 11, so the minimum center col is 11 (or 12).
- RIGHT: to col 21+3=24, 27, 30, 33, 36, 39, 42, 45, 48, 51... 51 is within [11, 52].
So from [27, 21], I can move UP to row 12 (5 UP moves: 27-15=12) and LEFT to col 12 (3 LEFT moves: 21-9=12).
Wait, 27 - 5*3 = 27 - 15 = 12. And 21 - 3*3 = 21 - 9 = 12. So the minimum safe center is [12, 12].
For collecting yellow dots:
- [17, 15]: Move LEFT 2 times to [27, 15]. Vertical bar at col 15 covers rows 16-38, which includes row 17. ✓
- [9, 6]: Move UP 6 times to [9, 15]. But 9 < 11, so this might cause game over. Let me try UP 5 times to [12, 15]. Horizontal bar at row 12, cols 1-24. This doesn't cover row 9.
  - Actually, the horizontal bar is at the center row. So from [12, 15], the horizontal bar is at row 12. The dot at [9, 6] is at row 9, which is not covered.
  - To cover [9, 6], I need the horizontal bar at row 9 or the vertical bar at col 6.
  - Vertical bar at col 6: from col 15, that's (15-6)/3 = 3 LEFT moves. Center at [12, 6]. But 6 < 11, so this might cause game over.
  - Horizontal bar at row 9: from row 12, that's (12-9)/3 = 1 UP move. Center at [9, 15]. But 9 < 11, so this might cause game over.
Hmm, it seems like I can't safely collect [9, 6] and [9, 24] without going off the board. But earlier, I collected blue dots by moving the blue cross to [24, 48], which is within the safe range.
Wait, let me reconsider the boundary. The blue cross has bars that are 13 pixels long. From center [24, 48], the horizontal bar spans cols 35-61. Col 61 is within the board (0-63). So the blue cross can extend to col 61.
For the yellow cross with bars that are 11 pixels long, from center [27, 21], the horizontal bar spans cols 10-32. Col 10 is within the board. So the yellow cross can extend to col 10.
But when I moved the yellow cross UP from [24, 15], the game over happened after 2 UP moves. Let me check: from [24, 15], 2 UP moves would put the center at [18, 15]. The vertical bar would span rows 7-29. Row 7 is within the board. So why did the game over happen?
Maybe the game over wasn't caused by the cross going off the board, but by something else. Let me look at the history more carefully.
Actually, I think the issue is that I moved the crosses in a way that caused them to overlap or merge incorrectly. Or maybe the game over was caused by the white pixel going off the board.
Let me try a different approach. Instead of trying to collect all yellow dots at once, let me collect them one by one, being careful about boundaries.
Current yellow cross center: [27, 21]
Yellow dots: [3, 15], [9, 6], [9, 24], [17, 15]
Plan:
1. Move LEFT 2 times to [27, 15]. Vertical bar at col 15 covers [17, 15].
2. Move UP 5 times to [12, 15]. Horizontal bar at row 12, cols 1-24. This doesn't cover [9, 6] or [9, 24].
   - But the vertical bar at col 15 spans rows 1-23. Row 9 is within 1-23, so the vertical bar covers [9, 15] but not [9, 6] or [9, 24].
3. To cover [9, 6] and [9, 24], I need the horizontal bar at row 9. From row 12, that's 1 more UP move to [9, 15]. But 9 < 11, so this might cause game over.
Let me try a different approach. What if I move the yellow cross to collect [9, 24] first (which is closer to the center), then [9, 6], then [17, 15], and finally [3, 15]?
From [27, 21]:
- To collect [9, 24]: Move UP 6 times to [9, 21]. Horizontal bar at row 9, cols 10-32. This covers [9, 24] (col 24 is within 10-32). But 9 < 11, so this might cause game over.
- Alternatively, move RIGHT 1 time to [27, 24]. Vertical bar at col 24 spans rows 16-38. Row 9 is not within 16-38, so this doesn't cover [9, 24].
Hmm, it seems like I can't collect [9, 24] without moving the horizontal bar to row 9, which would put the center at row 9, which is less than 11.
Wait, maybe the boundary is not [11, 52] but something else. Let me check the blue cross position. The blue cross is at [45, 36], and the horizontal bar spans cols 23-49. Col 23 is within the board. The vertical bar spans rows 32-58. Row 58 is within the board. So the blue cross is within the safe range.
But the yellow cross at [27, 21] has the horizontal bar spanning cols 10-32. Col 10 is within the board. So the yellow cross is also within the safe range.
Maybe the game over was caused by the cross going off the top of the board. From [24, 15], 2 UP moves would put the center at [18, 15]. The vertical bar spans rows 7-29. Row 7 is within the board. But maybe the game checks if the center is within a certain range, not the bars.
Let me try a more conservative approach. I'll move the yellow cross to collect [17, 15] first (which is safe), and see what happens.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the game over condition. The game over happened after I moved the yellow cross UP from [24, 15]. After 2 UP moves, the center would be at [18, 15]. But the game over happened. 
Looking at the current state after SPACE reset:
- Yellow cross center: [27, 21]
- Blue cross center: [45, 36]
The crosses are at different positions than before. This suggests that SPACE doesn't fully reset the level, or the crosses moved when the game over happened.
Let me try to collect the yellow dots more carefully. I'll start by collecting [17, 15] by moving LEFT 2 times.

# Let me try collecting [17, 15] first by moving LEFT 2 times
# Yellow cross center: [27, 21], move LEFT 2 to [27, 15]
# Vertical bar at col 15 spans rows 16-38, which includes row 17
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
